evals.prompt: Create a Terraform module for S3 with versioning, encryption, and lifecycle rules
evals.expected_output: A complete Terraform module with main.tf, variables.tf, outputs.tf, versions.tf
assertions: The output directory contains main.tf, variables.tf, outputs.tf, versions.tf
main.tf uses count = var.create_ pattern for conditional resources
variables.tf contains a 'name' variable of type string
variables.tf contains a 'tags' variable of type map(string)
outputs.tf uses try() for safe output values
No deprecated attributes (acl, versioning block) are used in main.tf
All resources have tags with merge() pattern
terraform validate passes on the generated module

evals.prompt: Create a basic S3 bucket module, just storage nothing fancy
evals.expected_output: A minimal module with only the essential S3 bucket resource
assertions: main.tf contains aws_s3_bucket but NOT complex resources like replication
variables.tf does not include optional features the user didn't request
Module still includes encryption by default (CIS requirement)
